title: dombeya australis subsp. geayi
intro: Dombeya australis subsp. geayi, also known as the 'Silver Bell', is a flowering plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Dombeya australis subsp. geayi are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
